title: 谋杀启事
summary:
During the investigation into the murder of Miss Mulgrew, the superintendent informs the group that while only Miss Simmons is currently being targeted by warnings, everyone has the right to legal counsel. Mrs. Swetnam provides a statement, initially claiming she was darning socks at four o'clock, but later clarifying that she spent time cleaning a blocked gutter in the rain from 4:15 until approximately 4:40. She offers the clean gutter as evidence of her task, though she has no witnesses. Edmund Swetnam states he was sleeping and did not hear his mother call him.

Mrs. Eastbrook initially claims she was in the study with Colonel Eastbrook listening to the radio, but the Colonel contradicts her, stating he was talking to a farmer named Mr. Lumpson from 3:45 until just before tea at 4:45. Consequently, Mrs. Eastbrook changes her story, admitting she went for a walk at 3:30. Chief Inspector Corrado explains that Miss McGarrett had identified a person she did not see in a room during a robbery, prompting Miss Chilcote to suggest Miss McGarrett saw everything from behind a door.

The proceedings are interrupted by Micky, the kitchen worker, who declares she is no longer willing to stay silent for money because she fears for her life. Micky testifies that on the night of the robbery, she was in the dining room and saw Miss Blacklock holding a gun next to a man. Coladock then interrupts to accuse Edmund of stealing Colonel Eastbrook's revolver and conspiring with Rudi Schlesinger. Coladock alleges that Edmund followed Patrick Simmons into the drawing room, shot Miss Blacklock, killed Rudi Schlesinger, and then returned to the room.
